mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS anchored near zero (0.08%), the near-zero cost of capital compresses equity discount rates and encourages risk-asset allocation, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as the effective funds rate remains below 0.25%.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.08 as of t-1mo, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) during any 12-month window in which FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25%, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in UNRATE that crosses below the prior 3-month average by more than 0.2pp signals accelerating labor market tightening, raising market expectations of imminent Fed QE tapering and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as rate-hike-timing risk repricing occurs.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls more than 0.2pp below its trailing 3-month average
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=7.3 as of t-1mo, declining from prior year highs
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive or flat mean 5-day forward return with p>0.10, or hit rate for negative returns falls below 45% over a rolling 18-month sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) jointly signals broad-based consumer demand strength that is not detectable from either series alone, driving above-trend corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=426563 and HOUST=896 both trending upward as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Joint acceleration episodes fail to produce 60-day forward returns exceeding the unconditional sample mean, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p>0.10) versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO during an early-stage industrial recovery signals expanding corporate output and margin recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month; this relationship inverts in late-cycle regimes where capacity constraints trigger inflation and tightening fears instead.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=98.9462 as of t-2mo, still below pre-crisis peak indicating early recovery
regime_hint: early-stage post-recession industrial recovery with low inflation
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum fails to produce above-median 20-day forward returns while CPIAUCSL remains below 2.5% y/y, or the sign of the effect flips within the low-inflation regime sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects expanding aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, translating into a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon regardless of the broader macro regime.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=136133 thousand as of t-1mo, showing continued monthly gains
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS prints are not significantly greater than following negative prints (t-test p>0.05) over a rolling 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above 2.5%, markets price in accelerated monetary tightening risk, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=233.318 as of t-2mo, implying y/y inflation still below 2.5% at snapshot
regime_hint: rising inflation regime approaching tightening threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns after threshold crossings are not significantly negative (p>0.05), or fewer than 50% of historical threshold-crossing episodes are followed by negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: The joint combination of near-zero FEDFUNDS and a declining UNRATE trend signals that the Fed is approaching a policy inflection point (tapering asset purchases), producing short-horizon negative S&P 500 returns as markets reprice rate-path uncertainty, an effect not present when either condition holds alone.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.08 and UNRATE=7.3 declining, consistent with 2013 taper-talk environment
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during joint low-FEDFUNDS/declining-UNRATE episodes are not significantly more negative than during episodes with only one condition present (p>0.10), or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing recovery regime, sustained month-over-month increases in HOUST signal strengthening construction and durable-goods-linked demand that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon; this relationship breaks down once mortgage-rate-driven regimes shift housing into contraction.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=896 thousand units as of t-2mo, near post-crisis highs
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following positive HOUST momentum are not significantly higher than baseline in the recovery regime, or the relationship's sign flips when tested outside identified housing recovery windows.
